what_it_does: Vitest test suite for navigate() and navigateFromQuery() in the recollection package. It builds a synthetic SQLite store, constructs the crossings graph, and verifies moves along the time and scale axes, boundary/empty-leaf behavior, k-limited fan-out, fail-loud handling of invalid axis/direction pairs, non-traversal of produced_by/references edges, and the recall-to-navigate chain in text mode.
observations: Each test creates a temp directory, sets TEST_DB_PATH, calls initDatabase(), seeds the same three-exchange session, runs buildCrossings(), and closes the database.
The seed helper inserts exchanges x1/x2/x3 under project projA and session sessA, plus a Write tool_call so a file node exists.
Constants define expected addresses: EX1/EX2/EX3, SESS, PROJ.
Tests time/next and time/prev along the precedes edges, asserting empty arrays past the ends.
Tests scale/out (exchange -> session -> project) and scale/in (project -> session -> ordered exchanges), including a k=2 limit.
Explicitly checks that navigating from the file node via time or scale returns [] because navigate only uses precedes/contains adjacency edges.
navigateFromQuery tests use mode=text to recall an entry address by phrase match, then apply time/next.
Invalid axis/direction combinations are tested by casting invalid values with as any and asserting thrown errors matching /unknown axis\/direction/.
standouts: The dedicated test that produced_by/references touch edges are never crossed by navigate.
Fail-loud registry discipline: unknown axis/direction pairs throw rather than silently returning empty.
navigateFromQuery integration covers the recall + move chain without invoking a model.
conventions: Uses Vitest describe/it/expect/beforeEach/afterEach with per-test database lifecycle.
Uses process.env.TEST_DB_PATH to influence initDatabase and cleans it up in afterEach.
Uses TypeScript as any casts to feed invalid enum values into typed navigate parameters.
URI-style address constants are declared at the top of the describe block.
concerns: Database open/seed/close is repeated in every test instead of being hoisted into beforeEach/afterEach.
Reliance on process.env.TEST_DB_PATH assumes initDatabase reads it; env mutation in tests is a local coupling.
as any casts for invalid inputs bypass compile-time checks and could mask API changes.
notes: A behavioral contract test that pins down how the address-mover walks the crossings graph and what edges it must ignore.
questions: What is the exact Hit object shape returned by navigate (kind, axis, direction, gist, etc.)?
How does buildCrossings represent precedes/contains edges in the database, and what ordering does navigate use?
What does navigateFromQuery do when recall returns multiple candidate addresses?
What are the intended semantics of k for singular moves such as time/next or scale/out?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it verifies navigate as an address-mover over the beat-3 crossings graph and the recall-to-navigate chain.
apparent_themes: Graph navigation over provenance with explicit axes and directions.
Strict API contracts with fail-loud validation.
Deterministic synthetic testing of memory-store behavior.
Separation of adjacency edges (precedes/contains) from touch edges (produced_by/references).
Model-free recall for navigation entry points.
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish a predictable, well-contracted mover over a structured memory graph, ensuring callers can only request valid axis/direction moves and that navigation stays on semantic containment and sequence edges rather than artifact-reference edges.
novelty: The explicit assertion that file nodes created by produced_by edges are unreachable through time/scale navigation, making the edge-type distinction testable.
contribution: A focused set of behavioral tests that document and enforce the expected moves, boundaries, error handling, and edge exclusions for navigate() and navigateFromQuery().
summary_for_embedding: A Vitest test file that exercises the navigate() address-mover and navigateFromQuery() recall-to-move chain over a synthetic SQLite crossings graph, covering time/scale axes, k-limits, fail-loud validation, and exclusion of produced_by/references edges.
intention_for_embedding: To lock down the contract of the recollection address-mover so that graph navigation behaves predictably and rejects invalid moves or unintended edge traversals.

symbols.does: Seeds a synthetic SQLite store with three ordered exchanges, a session, a project, and one Write tool_call for file-node testing.
symbols.description: The seed helper is a local test fixture that inserts exchanges x1, x2, and x3 into project projA and session sessA, then adds a produced_by-style tool_call so a file node exists. After seeding it calls buildCrossings() to construct the graph that navigate() traverses in the surrounding test cases.
